note: Phase 2 of issue #209: filtered report exports. Hand-authored mission. Fetch pre-built bugs.json on demand, filter in the browser with the same conservative <=25-sample logic as the on-screen view (filterBugsByExclusion), download filtered bugs.json + bugs.csv. Three WPs: factor bugsCsvTable (schema single-source), browser fetch/filter/download, docs. check:spec-kitty passes.

Browser fetch->filter->download. exclusionBox gains data-bugs-json (Accessibility page) + a hidden Filtered CSV/JSON row revealed by apply() only when a list is active and bugs.json is present. exclusionFilterScript: filterBugsData mirrors the on-screen conservative rule (drop only when complete && all excluded; else trim sample; scale counts for complete findings, keep counts for >25 samples); bugsToCsv mirrors bugsCsvTable headers + csvField escaping; fetch(bugsJsonHref) on click, download filtered .json (keeps archive shape + filtered_by) and .csv. Verified in real Chromium: all-medicare finding dropped, mixed finding trimmed 2->1 pages/4->2 instances, and the filtered CSV is byte-identical to server toCsv(bugsCsvTable(filtered)). npm run test:unit 276 pass; i18n template 587; i18n:check clean. FR-02/03/04, C-01/02/03/04, NFR-01/02 satisfied. Remaining: WP03 docs.

Docs. CLAUDE.md 'URL exclusion (three layers)' viewer paragraph extended with the filtered-download flow (fetch bugs.json on demand, <=25-sample rule matches on-screen, bugsCsvTable byte-parity, static files untouched). FEATURES.md viewer bullet adds the filtered-copy download. Docs-only; npm run test:unit 276 pass; i18n:check clean. FR-05 satisfied — all 12 criteria pass. Mission implementation complete; pending spec-kitty accept.

Post-merge fixes from issue #209 user feedback (control appeared to do nothing on cms.gov). (1) Relaxed the on-screen + download hide rule from complete-only to SAMPLE-BASED: a finding hides when every page the client can see matches, so >25-page findings (data-complete=0) actually hide; banner flags '(N matched on a sample)' with a view-everything reset. (2) Fixed dead config baseline: aggregate.js never passed target.url_exclude_patterns to renderAccessibilityPage, so url_exclude_patterns (e.g. cms.gov [.aspx]) was a no-op — now threaded. (3) Fixed misleading box help: recommend substring .aspx over anchored /\.aspx$/i (normalized URLs keep query strings, so $ misses .aspx?x=1). Added a render test for the excludePatterns wiring. Verified in Chromium: /medicare/ hides a 40-page all-medicare finding; .aspx hides aspx?query findings; filtered download consistent. npm run test:unit 277 pass; i18n:check clean.
